title: elaphoglossum mathewsii
intro: Elaphoglossum mathewsii, also known as the Mathews' Elaphoglossum, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate and lacy fronds. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum mathewsii include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. Elaphoglossum mathewsii is typically prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the dried fronds in hot water, or as a decoction by boiling the roots and stems in water.
